# Footguns

## Node.js >=20.0.0 enforced in package.json but not by npm install

When setting up CI or a new dev machine, run `node --version` before `npm test` or `npm start`. The `engines` field in package.json specifies `>=20.0.0`, but npm does not enforce this by default — it only prints a warning. The server may appear to start on Node 18, but the built-in test runner has API differences that cause subtle failures.
Consequence: CI pipelines pinned to Node 16 or 18 will produce confusing, version-unrelated test failures with no clear error message about the version mismatch.

_Source: `package.json`_

_Tags: `deployment`, `backend`_

## Install scripts use op- prefix in package.json but CLAUDE.md shows bare names

When invoking the installer directly, use `./scripts/op-install.sh` and `./scripts/op-uninstall.sh` (as shown in README), not `./scripts/install.sh` or `./scripts/uninstall.sh` (shown in the CLAUDE.md directory structure). The `npm run install-service` and `npm run uninstall-service` scripts in package.json reference the `op-` prefix paths. Copying the bare names from CLAUDE.md produces a 'No such file or directory' error with no hint about the rename.
Consequence: developers following the CLAUDE.md directory listing to invoke scripts directly will get a confusing error — always use the npm script aliases to avoid path drift.

_Source: `package.json`_

_Tags: `deployment`, `cli`_

## Node.js built-in test runner used — Jest API patterns fail silently

When writing or running tests, use Node's built-in `node:test` and `node:assert` modules, not Jest matchers. The `npm test` command runs `node --test test/*.test.js test/**/*.test.js` — the native runner introduced in Node 18 and stabilized in v20. Jest-style `expect()`, `beforeAll()`, and `afterAll()` calls produce no output and no errors; the tests simply don't run.
Consequence: installing Jest or copying Jest-style test patterns causes silent test coverage gaps — all test files must import from 'node:test' and 'node:assert'.

_Source: `package.json`_

_Tags: `testing`, `backend`_
